control_description: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets to meet the entity's objectives.

evidence_files.description: Nimbus Cloud Solutions physical security policy defining badge controls, visitor escort requirements, and termination procedures
HR-provided list of employees terminated during Q4 2025 with termination dates and badge return status
Badge access log for Q4 2025 with 60 entries covering all access points across the facility
Visitor sign-in log for Q4 2025 showing 30 visitor entries with escort status and areas visited

prompt: You are a SOC 2 auditor evaluating Nimbus Cloud Solutions' physical security controls for the Q4 2025 observation period (October 1 - December 31, 2025). Review the physical security policy, terminated employees list, badge access log, and visitor log. Assess whether CC6.4 requirements for restricting physical access are met. Cross-reference documents to identify any gaps where controls are not operating as designed.

findings.title: Terminated employee badge not deactivated — badge used 5 days after termination
findings.description: The terminated employees list shows Carlos Vega (badge B-3312) was terminated on October 28, 2025, with badge_return_status 'Not Returned.' Cross-referencing with the badge access log reveals two entries for B-3312 after the termination date: October 30 at 08:15 (AP-MAIN-01, General Office, Granted) and November 2 at 09:22 (AP-MAIN-01, General Office, Granted). Policy Section 4.5 requires badge deactivation within 4 business hours of HR termination notification. The badge was never deactivated, allowing a terminated employee physical access to the facility for at least 5 days after separation. The badge_return_status of 'Not Returned' confirms the physical badge was never collected either.
findings.expected_reasoning: The auditor should cross-reference the terminated employees list against the badge access log. Carlos Vega appears in the terminated list with a termination date of October 28. Searching the badge access log for his badge ID (B-3312) after October 28 reveals two successful badge swipes on October 30 and November 2. Policy requires deactivation within 4 business hours. Both the electronic deactivation and physical badge collection failed.

findings.title: Unescorted vendor accessed server room despite policy requiring escort for all non-employees
findings.description: The visitor log entry VIS-0118 shows vendor representative Mike Torres from CoolAir HVAC Services visited on November 14, 2025, with host employee listed as 'Derek Lam.' The areas_accessed field shows 'Server Room B' and the escort_status field shows 'Unescorted.' Policy Section 6.3 states 'All non-employees, including vendors and contractors, must be escorted by the designated host employee at all times while on premises. Under no circumstances shall a visitor or vendor be granted unescorted access to Restricted zones.' Server Room B is classified as a Restricted zone in Section 3.1. An unescorted vendor in the server room could access, photograph, or tamper with production infrastructure.
findings.expected_reasoning: The auditor should review the visitor log for escort compliance. Most visitors show 'Escorted' status, but VIS-0118 shows Mike Torres (CoolAir HVAC Services) with 'Unescorted' status in Server Room B. Cross-referencing with the policy confirms Section 6.3 requires escort for all non-employees, especially in Restricted zones. The vendor type does not exempt the escort requirement.
